title: Breaking Free: The Adventures of Dannen Lifehold

"'''Breaking Free: The Adventures of Dannen Lifehold'''" is a short story written by [[Dave Marron]] with illustrations by [[Doug Shuler]]. It was published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' ''1'']] in [[February]] [[1994]] by [[West End Games]].

It is set in the [[Rise of the Empire era]] before the destruction of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]].

==Plot summary==
The [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Dannen Lifehold]] has one last job before he can pay off his debt on his [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the [[Lifeline (YT-1300 light freighter)|''Lifeline'']], to the [[loan shark]] [[Linkaas]]. Lifehold arrives on [[Dohu VII]] to pick up this shipment, but, along the way, enters a [[Alcohol/Legends|drinking]] contest with the [[Silika]] [[Kenta Anwa]]. Thanks to Silika [[Water/Legends|water]] not affecting [[Human/Legends|humans]], Lifehold easily wins the contest and impresses the [[Cantina/Legends|bar]].

However, his celebration is short-lived as Linkaas's henchmen, [[Bakk]] and [[Chokk (Bloxian)|Chokk]], ambush him, intent on [[Death/Legends|killing]] him before he can settle his debt. Linkaas’s plan is to eliminate Lifehold, seize his ship, and sell it off to someone else.

Just as Lifehold is about to be killed, [[Klin Anwa]], Kenta’s brother, intervenes and saves him. Grateful for the help, Lifehold decides to make a hasty exit. On his way back to the ''Lifeline'', he meets [[Purr]], a genius [[Tinnell]] [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] who has upgraded his ship without being asked. With Linkaas's goons still on their tail, Lifehold flees to [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] for a fresh start.
